nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cornhill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name in SE England mainly from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumb) or one of the many minor places so called. It has often become   Cornell   and has sometimes been confused with   Cornwall  , and   Cornwell  , as in the case of Thomas Cornell, 1722 in   Parish Registers   (Horringer, Suffolk), who is also called Cornwall (1736), Cornwell (1740) and Cornhill (1766).
2  English:  see  Cornall  .
3  English:  see  Cornwall  .

variants.name: Cornwall
meanings: English:

habitational name from the county of Cornwall, which is named with the Old English tribal name Cornwealas. This is from Kernow (the term that the Cornish used to refer to themselves, a word of uncertain etymology, perhaps connected with a Celtic element meaning ‘horn, headland’), + Old English wealas ‘strangers, foreigners’, the term used by the Anglo-Saxons for British-speaking people.
variant of   Cornwell  .

variants.name: Parish Registers
meanings: English:  see  Lonsdale  .   Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorks Surnames   notes Tobias Londsdale or Lansdall, 1675–84 in   Parish Registers   (Keighley, WR Yorks).

variants.name: Cornwell
meanings: English:
habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, named from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

variant of Cornwall or Cornhill; in southeastern England mainly a habitational name from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumberland) or one of the many minor places so called, often becoming Cornell .

variants.name: Cornell
meanings: English: shortened form of Cornwell , Cornwall , or of Cornhill, a habitational name from a place in Northumberland named Cornhill, from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + halh ‘nook, recess’; or from Cornhill in London, a medieval grain exchange, named with Old English corn ‘corn, grain’ + hyll ‘hill’, or from some other place elsewhere similarly named.
English: variant of   Corney  .
English: possibly also a nickname from French corneille ‘rook, crow’, probably denoting a chatterer or someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

variants.name: Cornall
meanings: 1 English: the Lancs name is a variant of Corney , where -all and -ell are hypercorrect pronunciations of Cornah, Corner, and Cornoe, late pronunciations of Corney.

2 English: the Devon name is a variant of Cornwall . Here also probably belongs Gurnall, as in Joan Gurnall, 1586 in IGI (Crediton, Devon), and William Gurnall, 1650 in IGI (Lavenham, Suffolk). Compare Corney .

nameMeanings.name: Hubert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was popular among the Normans, who introduced it to Britain, where it was later reinforced by settlers from the Low Countries. An 8th-century St Hubert succeeded St Lambert as bishop of Maastricht and is regarded as the patron of hunters, since, like St Eustace , he is supposed to have seen a vision of Christ crucified between the antlers of a stag.

variants.name: Hupprecht
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. Hubert de Cornhill was born about 1050, in Cornhill on Tweed, Northumberland, England. She had at least 1 son with Hubert de Cornhill.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs. Hubert de Cornhill est née environ 1050, à Cornhill on Tweed, Northumberland, Angleterre. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Hubert de Cornhill.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs. Hubert de Cornhill wurde um 1050 in Cornhill on Tweed, Northumberland, England geboren. Sie hatte mit Hubert de Cornhill mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs. Hubert de Cornhill è nata il circa 1050 nel Cornhill on Tweed, Northumberland, England. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Hubert de Cornhill.

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. Hubert de Cornhill(은)는 대략 1050년 영국 노르스벌랜드 코른힐  트위드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Hubert de Cornhill(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs. Hubert de Cornhill nasceu aproximadamente 1050, em Cornhill on Tweed, Northumberland, Inglaterra. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Hubert de Cornhill.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs. Hubert de Cornhill родилась около 1050 г. в местности корнилл он твид, нортумбирланд, Англия. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Hubert de Cornhill.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs. Hubert de Cornhill nació aproximadamente 1050, en Cornhill on Tweed, Northumberland, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Hubert de Cornhill.
